# `aegis-boot` CLI synopsis

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `cli-docgen --write`. Do not edit directly. -->

This file is auto-generated from the live output of `aegis-boot <SUBCOMMAND> --help` — it is the authoritative usage + flags reference. For prose guides, examples, and exit-code narratives, see [`docs/CLI.md`](../CLI.md).

Phase 3b of [#286](https://github.com/williamzujkowski/aegis-boot/issues/286). Regenerate with:

```bash
cargo build -p aegis-cli --release
cargo run -p aegis-cli --bin cli-docgen --features docgen -- --write
```

---

## `aegis-boot add`

```text
aegis-boot add — copy an ISO onto the stick with verification

USAGE: aegis-boot add <iso-file> [/dev/sdX | /mnt/aegis-isos]
                  [--description TEXT] [--version VER] [--category CAT]

Optional sidecar metadata (#246) is written next to the ISO as
<iso>.aegis.toml so rescue-tui can show 'Network-install Debian 12'
instead of the bare filename. The sidecar is unsigned cosmetic
metadata — boot decisions still key off the sha256-attested manifest.
```

## `aegis-boot attest`

```text
aegis-boot attest — attestation receipts for flashed sticks

Each `aegis-boot flash` writes a JSON manifest recording exactly
what went onto the stick and the host environment that wrote it.
Manifests live in $XDG_DATA_HOME/aegis-boot/attestations/.

USAGE:
  aegis-boot attest list              List all stored attestations
  aegis-boot attest list --json       Machine-readable summary
  aegis-boot attest show <FILE>       Pretty-print one attestation
  aegis-boot attest show --json <FILE> Raw manifest JSON (full detail)
  aegis-boot attest --help            This message

EXAMPLES:
  aegis-boot attest list
  aegis-boot attest show ~/.local/share/aegis-boot/attestations/abc123-2026-04-16T12-34-56Z.json
  aegis-boot attest list --json | jq '.attestations[].flashed_at'
```

## `aegis-boot bug-report`

```text
aegis-boot bug-report — generate a bug-report bundle

USAGE:
  aegis-boot bug-report [--output PATH|-] [--format markdown|json]
                        [--no-redact --i-accept-pii-in-output]
                        [--dump-mapping PATH]
                        [--include-stick PATH]

OUTPUT MODES:
  --output -           Write markdown to stdout (default)
  --output PATH.md     Write markdown to file
  --output PATH.json   Write JSON to file
  --format markdown|json   Force format when extension is ambiguous

PRIVACY:
  Redaction is ON by default. Hostname, username, DMI + drive serials,
  MAC addresses and public IPv4 addresses are replaced with deterministic
  synthetic tokens (e.g. `host-ab12cd`, `serial-ef34gh`).
  --no-redact          Disable redaction (requires --i-accept-pii-in-output)
  --dump-mapping PATH  Write the real ↔ synthetic map to PATH. Keep it LOCAL —
                       it de-anonymizes any bundle you share.

EXAMPLES:
  aegis-boot bug-report                                # markdown to stdout
  aegis-boot bug-report --output report.md
  aegis-boot bug-report --output report.json --format json
  aegis-boot bug-report --include-stick /media/$USER/AEGIS_ISOS

(aegis-boot v0.15.0)
```

## `aegis-boot compat`

```text
aegis-boot compat — hardware compatibility lookup

USAGE:
  aegis-boot compat                     Show every known platform
  aegis-boot compat <query>             Fuzzy match one platform
  aegis-boot compat --my-machine        Auto-lookup from DMI (Linux only)
  aegis-boot compat --submit            Draft a hardware-report with DMI pre-fill
  aegis-boot compat --json [query]      Structured output

EXAMPLES:
  aegis-boot compat                     # full table
  aegis-boot compat thinkpad            # match by vendor or model
  aegis-boot compat --my-machine        # query this exact machine
  aegis-boot compat --submit            # pre-filled report URL
  aegis-boot compat --json              # script-friendly list

NOTES:
  Rows are verified outcomes only — no speculation. If your machine is missing,
  please submit a report:
  https://github.com/williamzujkowski/aegis-boot/issues/new?template=hardware-report.yml
```

## `aegis-boot completions`

```text
aegis-boot completions — emit shell completion scripts

USAGE:
  aegis-boot completions bash       # print bash completion to stdout
  aegis-boot completions zsh        # print zsh completion to stdout

INSTALL:
  # bash (persistent):
  aegis-boot completions bash | sudo tee /etc/bash_completion.d/aegis-boot

  # bash (transient, current shell only):
  source <(aegis-boot completions bash)

  # zsh:
  aegis-boot completions zsh > ~/.zsh/completions/_aegis-boot
```

## `aegis-boot doctor`

```text
aegis-boot doctor — host + stick health check

USAGE:
  aegis-boot doctor                      # human-readable table
  aegis-boot doctor --stick /dev/sdX     # include stick checks
  aegis-boot doctor --json               # machine-readable (CI / monitoring)

Reports a 0-100 health score with a single NEXT ACTION line.
Exit code 0 = healthy (PASS or only WARN); 1 = at least one FAIL.

JSON schema (stable, schema_version=1):
  { schema_version, tool_version, score, band, has_any_fail,
    next_action, rows: [{ verdict, name, detail }, ...] }
```

## `aegis-boot eject`

```text
aegis-boot eject — safely power-off and prepare a USB stick for removal

USAGE:
  aegis-boot eject [device]
  aegis-boot eject --help

BEHAVIOR:
  1. sync (flush dirty buffers)
  2. blockdev --flushbufs /dev/sdX (needs sudo)
  3. udisksctl power-off (polkit-friendly, no sudo) OR eject /dev/sdX

EXAMPLES:
  aegis-boot eject              # auto-detect removable drive
  aegis-boot eject /dev/sdc     # explicit device
```

## `aegis-boot fetch`

```text
aegis-boot fetch — download + verify a catalog ISO

USAGE:
  aegis-boot fetch <slug>
  aegis-boot fetch --out /path/to/dir <slug>
  aegis-boot fetch --no-gpg <slug>      # SHA-256 only (NOT recommended)
  aegis-boot fetch --dry-run <slug>     # Preview — print recipe, no downloads
  aegis-boot fetch --help

OPTIONS:
  --out DIR       Destination directory (default: $XDG_CACHE_HOME/aegis-boot/<slug>)
  --no-gpg        Skip GPG signature verification on SHA256SUMS
  --dry-run       Print what would be downloaded without doing it
  --no-progress   Suppress curl progress bar (for scripted usage / CI logs)
  --progress      Force progress bar even when stdout is not a TTY (rare)
  --help          This message

EXAMPLES:
  aegis-boot fetch ubuntu-24.04-live-server
  aegis-boot fetch --dry-run alpine-3.20-standard  # see URLs + sizes first
  aegis-boot fetch --out ~/Downloads alpine-3.20-standard

`aegis-boot fetch` does not write to a USB stick; it downloads + verifies
the ISO and prints the `aegis-boot add` command to copy it onto a stick.
```

## `aegis-boot fetch-image`

```text
aegis-boot fetch-image — download + verify a pre-built aegis-boot image

USAGE:
  aegis-boot fetch-image                               # latest release, cosign auto-verify
  aegis-boot fetch-image --version v0.15.0             # pin to a specific release
  aegis-boot fetch-image --url URL [--sha256 HEX]      # arbitrary URL

  --url URL       HTTPS URL of the aegis-boot.img to download (overrides --version)
  --version TAG   Pin to a specific release tag (e.g. v0.15.0)
  --out PATH      Where to write the image (default: $XDG_CACHE_HOME/aegis-boot/)
  --sha256 HEX    Required sha256; mismatch deletes the download + exits 1
  --no-cosign     Skip the cosign keyless signature check (air-gap, offline)

VERIFICATION:
  sha256:  required-if-passed; prints computed hash when omitted
  cosign:  auto-attempted; downloads <URL>.sig + <URL>.pem, runs
           `cosign verify-blob` against aegis-boot's release-workflow OIDC
           identity. Graceful-degrades when signatures aren't published.

Composes with `flash`:
  img=$(aegis-boot fetch-image) && aegis-boot flash --image "$img" /dev/sdX
```

## `aegis-boot flash`

```text
aegis-boot flash — write aegis-boot to a USB stick

USAGE: aegis-boot flash [DEVICE] [--dry-run] [--yes] [--image PATH] [--no-progress]
                       [--direct-install [--out-dir PATH]]
  No DEVICE        = auto-detect removable drives.
  /dev/sdX (Linux) or /dev/diskN (macOS) = flash to that drive.
  --dry-run        = print the typed Plan of operations and exit
                     before any destructive action. Useful for
                     pre-flight review or CI dry-runs. (#247)
  --yes / -y       = skip the 'type flash to confirm' prompt (DESTRUCTIVE).
  --image PATH     = write a pre-built image instead of invoking mkusb.sh.
                     Required on macOS (mkusb.sh is Linux-only).
  --no-progress    = suppress the indicatif progress bar during dd. Use in
                     CI, pipes, or dumb terminals where the \r-updated bar
                     would render as a long noisy line. (#244 PR3)
  --no-expand      = skip auto-expand of AEGIS_ISOS to fill the target device
                     post-flash. Without this flag, a fresh stick gets the full
                     device as its ISO partition (#242). Rare — use when you
                     deliberately want the small mkusb-default partition size.
  --direct-install = bypass mkusb.sh + dd. Partition + format + stage the signed
                     boot chain in place via sgdisk + mkfs.{fat,exfat} + mtools.
                     ~30 sec vs ~4 min on USB 2.0. Linux only. (#274 Phase 3)
                     Mutually exclusive with --image.
  --out-dir PATH   = directory holding the aegis-boot initramfs.cpio.gz for
                     --direct-install. Default: ./out (mkusb.sh convention).
```

## `aegis-boot init`

```text
aegis-boot init — one-command rescue stick (flash + fetch + add)

USAGE:
  aegis-boot init [/dev/sdX] [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
  --profile NAME     Profile to install (default: panic-room)
  --yes, -y          Skip interactive confirmations (destructive); also
                     skips the wizard's serial-confirmation gate (#245)
  --force            Skip the wizard's 'device is currently mounted' gate
  --no-doctor        Skip doctor preflight (not recommended)
  --no-gpg           Skip GPG verification on fetched ISOs
  --list-profiles    Print profile names, one per line (for completion)
  --help, -h         This message

INTERACTIVE MODE (no /dev/sdX, no --yes):
  The serial-confirmation wizard guards against wrong-device dd: pick a
  USB stick from a numbered list, see its hardware serial, type the
  last 4 chars to confirm. (#245) See docs/HOW_IT_WORKS.md for the
  full trust narrative.

PROFILES:
  panic-room        Emergency recovery kit — Alpine 3.20 + Ubuntu 24.04 Server + Rocky 9
  minimal           Fastest rescue stick — Alpine 3.20 only (~200 MiB)
  server            Enterprise server rescue — Ubuntu 24.04 Server + Rocky 9 + AlmaLinux 9

EXAMPLES:
  aegis-boot init                       # auto-detect drive, panic-room
  aegis-boot init /dev/sdc              # explicit device
  aegis-boot init /dev/sdc --yes        # unattended
  aegis-boot init --profile minimal     # fastest — Alpine only
  aegis-boot init --profile server      # Ubuntu Server + Rocky + Alma
```

## `aegis-boot list`

```text
aegis-boot list — inventory ISOs on the stick

USAGE:
  aegis-boot list [/dev/sdX | /mnt/aegis-isos]
  aegis-boot list --json [target]   # machine-readable output

  No target argument = auto-find the mounted AEGIS_ISOS partition.
```

## `aegis-boot man`

```text
aegis-boot man — emit the aegis-boot(1) man page to stdout

USAGE:
  aegis-boot man                              # print to stdout
  aegis-boot man | sudo tee \
    /usr/local/share/man/man1/aegis-boot.1 > /dev/null

After installing:
  sudo mandb -q                               # refresh man index
  man aegis-boot                              # test it

See also: `aegis-boot completions bash` / `zsh` for shell completions.
```

## `aegis-boot quickstart`

```text
aegis-boot quickstart — shortest path from stick to booted rescue

USAGE: aegis-boot quickstart /dev/sdX

Flashes the stick via --direct-install, fetches Alpine 3.20 Standard
(~200 MiB) from the signed catalog, and stages it — one command from
stick-in-hand to booted rescue-tui.

Behavior:
  * The device arg is REQUIRED. Explicit path — no auto-detect.
  * Equivalent to:  aegis-boot init --profile minimal --yes \
                                   --direct-install /dev/sdX
  * --direct-install is ~8x faster than the legacy dd path on USB 2.0.

For a different ISO, use:
  * aegis-boot init --profile panic-room /dev/sdX   # 3 ISOs, 5 GiB
  * aegis-boot init --profile server     /dev/sdX   # 3 server distros
  * aegis-boot flash /dev/sdX && aegis-boot add <slug>  # fine-grained

Related: #352 (sub-10-minute flash-to-boot epic).
```

## `aegis-boot recommend`

```text
aegis-boot recommend — curated ISO catalog

USAGE:
  aegis-boot recommend               List all catalog entries (human table)
  aegis-boot recommend <slug>        Show download + verify recipe
  aegis-boot recommend --slugs-only  One slug per line (for shell completion)
  aegis-boot recommend --json [slug] Full entry details as JSON

EXAMPLES:
  aegis-boot recommend
  aegis-boot recommend ubuntu-24.04-live-server
  aegis-boot recommend --json | jq '.entries[].slug'
```

## `aegis-boot tour`

```text
aegis-boot tour — 30-second in-terminal walkthrough

USAGE: aegis-boot tour

Prints the 4-command path to a working rescue stick, plus
pointers to docs/HOW_IT_WORKS.md and docs/TOUR.md. No flags.
```

## `aegis-boot update`

```text
aegis-boot update — in-place signed-chain update (read-only check for now)

USAGE:
  aegis-boot update <device>
  aegis-boot update --help

BEHAVIOR (phase 1 of #181):
  Validates that the target stick is a known aegis-boot stick and
  that its attestation manifest matches the disk GUID. Reports
  ELIGIBLE / NOT ELIGIBLE with a specific reason. Does NOT write.

  The actual atomic in-place update lands in follow-up PRs — see
  issue #181 for the phased plan.

WHY YOU'D USE THIS (once full update ships):
  - Apply shim/GRUB/kernel CVE fixes without wiping AEGIS_ISOS
  - Rotate signing keys on the boot chain
  - Bump aegis-boot releases without losing your ISO inventory
```

## `aegis-boot verify`

```text
aegis-boot verify — re-verify every ISO on a stick against its sidecar checksum

USAGE:
  aegis-boot verify                 Auto-find mounted AEGIS_ISOS
  aegis-boot verify /dev/sdX        Mount partition 2 and verify
  aegis-boot verify /mnt/iso-dir    Use explicit mount path
  aegis-boot verify --json [target] Machine-readable (CI / monitoring)
  aegis-boot verify --help

EXIT CODES:
  0  every ISO verified (or only NotPresent verdicts — no sidecars)
  1  any Mismatch / Forged / Unreadable verdict
  2  invalid arguments or could not resolve the target

RELATED:
  aegis-boot list      — view verification state + attestation summary
  aegis-boot add       — copy + verify a new ISO onto the stick
  (In rescue-tui, press `v` on an ISO for the in-boot re-verification.)
```
